texto: Books are like people: fascinating, inspiring, thought-provoking, some laugh, some meditate, others ache with old age, but still have wisdom: some are disease-ridden, some deceitful; but others are a delight to behold, and many travel to foreign lands; some cry, some teach, others are lots of fun, they are excellent companions and all have individuality - Books are friends. What person has too many friends?
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Gladys M. Hunt: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “Books are like people: fascinating, inspiring, thought-provoking, some laugh, some meditate, othe…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, books, others pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de viagem e inspiracao.

texto: Grief is like a journey one must take on a winding mountainside, often seeing the same scenery many times, a road which eventually leads to somewhere we've never been before.

texto: What is home? My favorite definition is "a safe place," a place where one is free from attack, a place where one experiences secure relationships and affirmation. It's a place where people share and understand each other. Its relationships are nurturing. The people in it do not need to be perfect; instead, they need to be honest, loving, supportive, recognizing a common humanity that makes all of us vulnerable.
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de Gladys M. Hunt que provoca temas ligados a familia. Ao questionar “What is home? My favorite definition is "a safe place," a place where one is free from attack, a …”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre familia, family, home, com foco em home e love.

texto: This savoring of life is no small thing. The element of wonder is almost lost today with the onslaught of the media and gadgets of our noisy world. To let a child lose it is to make him blind and deaf to the best of life.
